task_completion.description: Create and save a dashboard named 'Workload Burstiness Index'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite dashboard UI should be visible with the title 'Workload Burstiness Index' displayed at the top.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Compute Thrashing' graph with the required raw metric and alias.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel titled 'Compute Thrashing' is visible. Its legend must display the raw metric 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' and the alias 'CPU Volatility'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Database Thrashing' graph with the required raw metric and alias.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel titled 'Database Thrashing' is visible. Its legend must display the raw metric 'servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization' and the alias 'DB Volatility'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Network Burstiness' graph with the required raw metric and alias.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel titled 'Network Burstiness' is visible. Its legend must display the raw metric 'servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in' and the alias 'Network Volatility'.

task_completion.description: Apply derivative, absolute, and movingAverage (window 10) functions to the volatility metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the metric composer or graph edit dialog (visible in trajectory screenshots), the metric definition must show the nested functions 'absolute(derivative(...))' and 'movingAverage(..., 10)' applied to the respective raw metrics.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to construct the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Graphite web UI (browser window, clicking through metric trees, using the composer), rather than just writing a JSON file in a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual plotted time-series data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panels should show actual lines/curves representing the data, confirming the metrics are successfully querying the backend, not just empty graphs or mock images.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-made JSON dashboard definition.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots should show the step-by-step construction of the graphs, adding metrics, and applying functions in the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Workload Burstiness Index'. It must contain exactly three graphs: 1) 'Compute Thrashing' plotting 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' and an alias 'CPU Volatility'. 2) 'Database Thrashing' plotting 'servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization' and an alias 'DB Volatility'. 3) 'Network Burstiness' plotting 'servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in' and an alias 'Network Volatility'. Each volatility metric must be constructed using the nested functions 'absolute(derivative(metric))' smoothed by a 'movingAverage' with a window of exactly 10.
